fundamentals.name: Applied Optoelectronics, Inc.
fundamentals.longBusinessSummary: Applied Optoelectronics, Inc. engages in the design, manufacture, and sale of fiber-optic networking products in the United States, Taiwan, and China. The company offers optical modules, optical filters, lasers, laser components, subassemblies, transmitters and transceivers, turn-key equipment, headend, node, and distribution equipment, as well as amplifiers. It sells its products to internet data center operators, cable television, telecom equipment manufacturers, fiber-to-the-home, and internet service providers through its direct and indirect sales channels. Applied Optoelectronics, Inc. was incorporated in 1997 and is headquartered in Sugar Land, Texas.

judgment.summary: Rapidly growing company with high revenue expansion but currently unprofitable and trading at a high forward P/E.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last quarter missed EPS estimates (-0.07 vs -0.05); next earnings date is 2026-08-06.
positives: Revenue growth 51.4% YoY — exceptional top-line expansion.
Analyst growth est, current Q: 109.4% — strong expected earnings acceleration.
Analyst growth est, current FY: 441.7% — massive projected earnings growth.
Current ratio 3.83 and Quick ratio 2.85 — strong short-term liquidity.
PEG ratio 0.78 — suggests growth may be undervalued relative to its P/E.
negatives: Operating margin -8.6% and Profit margin -8.5% — the company is not yet profitable.
Return on equity -6.1% and Return on assets -3.3% — poor profitability metrics.
Free cash flow (TTM): $-445.58M — burning cash significantly.
Forward P/E: 38.03 — high multiple for an unprofitable company.
Debt/Equity: 25.36 — high leverage relative to equity.

news.paragraph: Applied Optoelectronics (AAOI) is experiencing strong demand from data centers and AI computing, highlighted by a significant upgrade deal with Mediacom. Despite this positive demand signal, the stock saw a notable dip recently, alongside other optics players, as the sector cooled. The company's growth trajectory is impressive, with substantial analyst estimates for current quarter and full-year earnings, though it remains unprofitable on an operating and net basis.
